levels.title: Learn pico-fastapi
levels.description: Build REST APIs with dependency-injected controllers.

levels.title: Your First Controller
levels.description:
Your First Controller

pico-fastapi lets you define REST controllers as classes managed by the pico-ioc container. You mark a class with the @controller decorator and specify a URL prefix.

In controllers.py, the HealthController class is missing its @controller decorator. Add @controller(prefix="/api") so that pico-fastapi registers it as a REST controller.

In main.py, we initialize the container and verify the controller was registered.

files.name: controllers.py
main.py
files.content:
import pico_ioc as pico
import pico_fastapi
import controllers

container = pico.init(modules=[pico_fastapi, controllers])

# Verify the controller is registered in the container
ctrl = container.get(controllers.HealthController)
print(f"Controller found: {type(ctrl).__name__}")
print(f"health_check result: {ctrl.health_check()}")

hints: The controller class needs a decorator to tell pico-fastapi it is a REST controller.
Import `controller` from `pico_fastapi` and apply it to the class with a `prefix` argument.
Add `@controller(prefix="/api")` directly above the `class HealthController:` line.

levels.title: Route Decorators
levels.description:
Route Decorators

pico-fastapi provides route decorators that mirror FastAPI: @get, @post, @put, and @delete. Each takes a path string and optional keyword arguments like status_code.

The TodoController in controllers.py has methods but is missing route decorators. Add the correct decorator to each method.

The test file checks that each method has the expected route metadata attached by the decorators.

files.name: controllers.py
test_main.py
files.content:
import pico_ioc as pico
import pico_fastapi
import controllers

pico.init(modules=[pico_fastapi, controllers])

def test_list_has_get_route():
    meta = getattr(controllers.TodoController.list_todos, "_route_meta", None)
    assert meta is not None, "list_todos is missing a route decorator"
    assert meta["method"] == "GET"
    assert meta["path"] == "/"

def test_create_has_post_route():
    meta = getattr(controllers.TodoController.create_todo, "_route_meta", None)
    assert meta is not None, "create_todo is missing a route decorator"
    assert meta["method"] == "POST"
    assert meta["status_code"] == 201

def test_update_has_put_route():
    meta = getattr(controllers.TodoController.update_todo, "_route_meta", None)
    assert meta is not None, "update_todo is missing a route decorator"
    assert meta["method"] == "PUT"
    assert meta["path"] == "/{todo_id}"

def test_delete_has_delete_route():
    meta = getattr(controllers.TodoController.delete_todo, "_route_meta", None)
    assert meta is not None, "delete_todo is missing a route decorator"
    assert meta["method"] == "DELETE"
    assert meta["status_code"] == 204

hints: Each method needs a route decorator from `pico_fastapi` that matches the HTTP verb it represents.
`@get("/")` for listing, `@post("/", status_code=201)` for creating. The path goes inside the parentheses.
The complete set is: `@get("/")`, `@post("/", status_code=201)`, `@put("/{todo_id}")`, `@delete("/{todo_id}", status_code=204)`.

levels.title: Injecting Services
levels.description:
Injecting Services into Controllers

Controllers are pico-ioc components, so they support constructor injection just like any other component. If a controller's __init__ declares a type-hinted parameter, the container will resolve and inject it automatically.

In services.py, a GreetingService component is defined. The GreetingController in controllers.py needs this service but its __init__ is missing the parameter.

Add greeting_service: GreetingService to the constructor and store it as self._service.

files.name: services.py
controllers.py
main.py
files.content:
import pico_ioc as pico
import pico_fastapi
import services
import controllers

container = pico.init(modules=[pico_fastapi, services, controllers])

ctrl = container.get(controllers.GreetingController)
result = ctrl.greet("World")
print(f"Response: {result}")

# Verify the service was injected
assert hasattr(ctrl, '_service'), "Controller is missing _service attribute"
assert isinstance(ctrl._service, services.GreetingService), "Wrong type injected"
print("Service injection verified!")

hints: The `__init__` method needs to accept a parameter so the container can inject the service.
Add a type-hinted parameter: `def __init__(self, greeting_service: GreetingService):` and assign it to `self._service`.
The complete constructor is: `def __init__(self, greeting_service: GreetingService):\n        self._service = greeting_service`

levels.title: Response Models with Pydantic
levels.description:
Response Models

FastAPI uses Pydantic models to validate and document API responses. In pico-fastapi, you pass response_model to the route decorator so that FastAPI knows the shape of the returned data.

schemas.py defines two Pydantic models. The routes in controllers.py are missing their response_model parameter. Add the correct model to each route decorator.

The test verifies that the route metadata includes the expected response model.

files.name: schemas.py
files.content:
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import List

class ItemResponse(BaseModel):
    id: int
    name: str
    price: float

class ItemListResponse(BaseModel):
    items: List[ItemResponse]
    total: int

files.name: controllers.py
test_main.py
files.content:
import pico_ioc as pico
import pico_fastapi
import controllers
import schemas

pico.init(modules=[pico_fastapi, controllers])

def test_list_items_response_model():
    meta = getattr(controllers.ItemController.list_items, "_route_meta", None)
    assert meta is not None, "list_items is missing a route decorator"
    assert meta.get("response_model") is schemas.ItemListResponse

def test_get_item_response_model():
    meta = getattr(controllers.ItemController.get_item, "_route_meta", None)
    assert meta is not None, "get_item is missing a route decorator"
    assert meta.get("response_model") is schemas.ItemResponse

def test_create_item_response_model():
    meta = getattr(controllers.ItemController.create_item, "_route_meta", None)
    assert meta is not None, "create_item is missing a route decorator"
    assert meta.get("response_model") is schemas.ItemResponse
    assert meta.get("status_code") == 201

hints: Each route decorator accepts a `response_model` keyword argument, just like FastAPI's own decorators.
For the list endpoint use `@get("/", response_model=ItemListResponse)`. The single-item endpoints use `ItemResponse`.
The complete decorators are: `@get("/", response_model=ItemListResponse)`, `@get("/{item_id}", response_model=ItemResponse)`, and `@post("/", status_code=201, response_model=ItemResponse)`.

levels.title: Multi-Layer Architecture
levels.description:
Multi-Layer Architecture

Real applications follow a layered pattern: Controller -> Service -> Repository. Each layer has a single responsibility and depends only on the layer below it.

This lab has four files with missing pieces. The UserRepository needs the @component decorator, the UserService needs its repository dependency injected, and the UserController needs the @controller decorator and its service dependency.

Wire all the layers together so the test passes.

files.name: repository.py
services.py
controllers.py
test_main.py
files.content:
import pico_ioc as pico
import pico_fastapi
import repository
import services
import controllers

container = pico.init(modules=[pico_fastapi, repository, services, controllers])

def test_controller_is_registered():
    ctrl = container.get(controllers.UserController)
    assert ctrl is not None

def test_service_is_injected():
    ctrl = container.get(controllers.UserController)
    assert hasattr(ctrl, '_service')
    assert isinstance(ctrl._service, services.UserService)

def test_repository_is_injected():
    svc = container.get(services.UserService)
    assert hasattr(svc, '_repo')
    assert isinstance(svc._repo, repository.UserRepository)

def test_list_users_returns_data():
    ctrl = container.get(controllers.UserController)
    users = ctrl.list_users()
    assert len(users) == 2
    assert users[0]["name"] == "Alice"

def test_get_user_by_id():
    ctrl = container.get(controllers.UserController)
    user = ctrl.get_user(1)
    assert user["name"] == "Alice"

hints: Three things are broken: the repository needs `@component`, the service needs its dependency, and the controller needs both `@controller` and its dependency.
In `repository.py` add `@component` above the class. In `services.py` change `__init__` to accept `repo: UserRepository` and assign `self._repo = repo`.
In `controllers.py` add `@controller(prefix="/api/users")` above the class and change `__init__` to accept `service: UserService` with `self._service = service`.
